title: Getting started with RGraphSpace
text: RGraphSpace R package generates ggplot2 graphics igraph objects (Csardi Nepusz 2006), scaling nodes edges unit space. package implements new ggplot2 geometric prototypes (Wickham 2016), optimized representing large networks. enables extensive customization aesthetics visual style, including colors, shapes, line types. extended documentation use cases, see online tutorials.

title: Getting started with RGraphSpace
text: section create toy igraph object demonstrate basic RGraphSpace workflow. input data configured manually ensure users can easily view basic graph attributes. use igraph’s make_star() function create simple star-like graph V() E() functions set attributes vertices edges, respectively. RGraphSpace require vertices x, y, name attributes.
code: #--- Load required packages library("igraph") library("ggplot2") library("RGraphSpace") # Make a 'toy' igraph with 5 nodes and 4 edges; # ..either a directed or undirected graph gtoy1 <- make_star(5, mode="out")  # Check whether the graph is directed or not is_directed(gtoy1) ## [1] TRUE  # Check graph size vcount(gtoy1) ## [1] 5 ecount(gtoy1) ## [1] 4  # Assign 'x' and 'y' coordinates to each vertex; # ..this can be an arbitrary unit in (-Inf, +Inf) V(gtoy1)$x <- c(0, 2, -2, -4, -8) V(gtoy1)$y <- c(0, 0,  2, -4,  0)  # Assign a name to each vertex V(gtoy1)$name <- paste0("n", 1:5) # Plot the 'gtoy1' using standard R graphics plot(gtoy1) # Plot the 'gtoy1' using RGraphSpace plotGraphSpace(gtoy1, add.labels = TRUE)

title: Getting started with RGraphSpace
text: Next, list vertex edge attributes can passed RGraphSpace methods.

title: Getting started with RGraphSpace
code: # Node size (numeric in [0, 100], as '%' of the plot space) V(gtoy1)$nodeSize <- c(8, 5, 5, 10, 5)  # Node shape (integer code between 0 and 25; see 'help(points)') V(gtoy1)$nodeShape <- c(21, 22, 23, 24, 25)  # Node color (Hexadecimal or color name) V(gtoy1)$nodeColor <- c("red", "#00ad39", "grey80", "lightblue", "cyan")  # Node line width (as in 'lwd' standard graphics; see 'help(gpar)') V(gtoy1)$nodeLineWidth <- 1  # Node line color (Hexadecimal or color name) V(gtoy1)$nodeLineColor <- "grey20"  # Node labels ('NA' will omit labels) V(gtoy1)$nodeLabel <- c("V1", "V2", "V3", "V4", NA)  # Node label size (in pts) V(gtoy1)$nodeLabelSize <- 8  # Node label color (Hexadecimal or color name) V(gtoy1)$nodeLabelColor <- "black"  # Node transparency (in [0,1]) V(gtoy1)$nodeAlpha <- 1

title: Getting started with RGraphSpace
text: Given list edges, RGraphSpace represents one edge pair connected vertices. multiple edges connecting node pair, display attributes first occurrence data.
code: # Edge width (as in 'lwd' standard graphics; see 'help(gpar)') E(gtoy1)$edgeLineWidth <- 0.8  # Edge color (Hexadecimal or color name) E(gtoy1)$edgeLineColor <- c("red","green","blue","black")  # Edge type (as in 'lty' standard graphics; see 'help(gpar)') E(gtoy1)$edgeLineType <- c("solid", "11", "dashed", "2124")  # Edge transparency (in [0,1]) E(gtoy1)$edgeAlpha <- 1

title: Getting started with RGraphSpace
text: Arrowhead directed graphs: default, arrow drawn edge according left--right orientation edge list (e.g. -> B). mutual connections, package recode mutual edges represent bidirectional flow. Arrowhead undirected graphs: default, arrow drawn undirected graphs. However, arrowheads may assigned according coding . … plot updated igraph object. straightforward call simply passes igraph directly wrapper plotGraphSpace() function.
code: # Arrowhead types in directed graphs ## Integer or character code: ## 0 = "---", 1 = "-->", -1 = "--|" E(gtoy1)$arrowType <- 1 # Arrowhead types in undirected graphs ## Integer or character code: ##  0 = "---" ##  1 = "-->",  2 = "<--",  3 = "<->",  4 = "|->", ## -1 = "--|", -2 = "|--", -3 = "|-|", -4 = "<-|",  E(gtoy1)$arrowType <- 1 # Note: in undirected graphs, this attribute overrides  # the edge's orientation in the edge list # Plot the updated 'gtoy1' using RGraphSpace plotGraphSpace(gtoy1, add.labels = TRUE)

title: Getting started with RGraphSpace
text: Alternatively, igraph object can transformed GraphSpace container passed underlying geoms:
code: gs <- GraphSpace(gtoy1) gs <- normalizeGraphSpace(gs) ggplot(gs) +    geom_edgespace() +   geom_nodespace() +   theme_gspace_coords(is_norm = TRUE)

title: Customizing aesthetics
text: section illustrates RGraphSpace integrates ggplot2 using geoms building blocks (Wickham 2016). Graph attributes stored within GraphSpace object can handled two ways: Identity mapping: Graph attributes interpreted “identity values” (nodeColor, nodeSize, nodeShape) displayed exactly , without scaling mapping. Dynamic aesthetic mapping: Graph attributes mapped aesthetics (colour, size, shape) rendered standard ggplot2 scales, automatically generate synchronized legends. facilitate integration, RGraphSpace implements three specialized geoms designed handle graph data types within ggplot2 workflow. geoms use dual-anchor normalization approach align layers, critical analysis network elements must stay accurately referenced spatial map image. geom_graphspace(): high-level convenience layer processes nodes edges single call. geom_nodespace(): Dedicated rendering nodes. Inherits GeomPoint aesthetic mappings, modified inform edge layer node states. can used inject_nodespace() function adjust edge offsets size aesthetic applied nodes. geom_edgespace(): Handles relational data nodes. Inherits GeomSegment aesthetic mappings; unlike standard segments, “node-aware” dynamically calibrates start end points.

title: Customizing aesthetics
text: following example, create small modular graph containing variables different types demonstrate RGraphSpace geoms handle different mapping requirements.
code: library("igraph") library("ggplot2") library("RGraphSpace")  # Make a toy modular graph gtoy2 <- sample_islands(   islands.n = 3,       # number of modules   islands.size = 30,   # nodes per module   islands.pin = 0.25,  # probability of edges within modules   n.inter = 2)         # edges between modules  # Assign module membership to nodes V(gtoy2)$module <- rep(1:3, each = 30)  # Assign colors to nodes V(gtoy2)$nodeColor <- rainbow(3)[V(gtoy2)$module]  # Assign a categorical variable to nodes V(gtoy2)$node_group <- c("A", "B", "C")[V(gtoy2)$module]  # Assign numeric variables to nodes and edges V(gtoy2)$node_var <- runif(vcount(gtoy2)) E(gtoy2)$edge_var <- runif(ecount(gtoy2))  # Create a GraphSpace from the toy igraph gs <- GraphSpace(gtoy2)

title: Customizing aesthetics
text: example, nodeColor already contains final colour values stored GraphSpace object. colours displayed -geom_graphspace() function. approach useful nodes pre-processed specific color schemes want visual output without mapping.  trade-approach , one hand, attributes reflect original data directly, legend accessible. identity scales bypass legend-building process ggplot2. legend required explain meaning colors, attribute mapped via aesthetics (e.g., aes(fill = attribute)) modified scale_*() function.
code: ggplot() +    geom_graphspace(colour = "grey", data = gs) +   theme(aspect.ratio = 1)

title: Customizing aesthetics
text: example, node categorical variable node_group mapped fill aesthetic. call geom_edgespace() geom_nodespace() independent layers provide maximum flexibility mapping aesthetics legends.
code: ggplot(gs) + geom_edgespace() +   geom_nodespace(aes(fill = node_group), colour = "grey") +   scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis") +   theme_gspace_coords()

title: Customizing aesthetics
text: example, node edge numeric variables mapped fill colour aesthetics, respectively.
code: # Map aesthetics to numeric variables ggplot(gs) +    geom_edgespace(aes(colour = edge_var)) +   geom_nodespace(aes(fill = node_var), colour = "grey") +   scale_colour_continuous(palette = c("cyan","blue")) +   scale_fill_continuous(palette = c("white","purple")) +   theme_gspace_coords()

title: Customizing aesthetics
text: need map different variables aesthetic (colour) independent scales, ggnewscale package offers elegant solution introduce additional scales within plot (Campitelli 2025); example:
code: if (!require("ggnewscale", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("ggnewscale") } library("ggnewscale") ggplot(data = gs) +    geom_edgespace(aes(colour = edge_var)) +   scale_colour_continuous(palette = c("cyan","blue")) +   ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() +   geom_nodespace(aes(colour = node_var),      stroke = 2, fill = NA) +   scale_colour_continuous(palette = c("white","purple")) +   theme_gspace_coords()

title: Interactive visualization
text: static plots essential documentation publication, interactive visualization allows us explore local neighborhoods global architecture network real-time. connecting RGraphSpace specialized interactive tools, can move beyond fixed layouts dynamic manipulation. following example demonstrates interoperability RGraphSpace RedeR, R/Bioconductor package interactive network visualization manipulation.
code: # Install RedeR, a graph package for interactive visualization if(!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages("BiocManager") } if(!require("RedeR", quietly = TRUE)){   BiocManager::install("RedeR") }

title: Interactive visualization
text: RedeR need Java Runtime Environment (JRE; version >=11). ensure environment ready, can check installed Java version directly R:
code: library("RedeR") RedPort(checkJava=TRUE) # RedeR will need Java Runtime Environment (Java >=11) # Checking Java version installed on this system... # openjdk version "21.0.10" 2026-01-20 # OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 21.0.10+7-Ubuntu-124.04) # OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM (build 21.0.10+7-Ubuntu-124.04, mixed mode, sharing)  # Note: The output may vary, but ensure your system meets the minimum requirement.

title: Interactive visualization
text: jumping interactive interface, can use standard RGraphSpace call verify graph’s structure current layout. provides baseline comparison start modifying network dynamically.  main advantage interoperability comes “round-trip” workflow. can send graph space RedeR, use force-directed algorithms manually refine layout, pull updated geometry back R analysis plotting ggplot2. following steps demonstrate launch, manipulation, retrieval process:
code: library("RedeR") library("RGraphSpace") library("igraph") data(gtoy1, package = "RGraphSpace") plotGraphSpace(gtoy1, add.labels = TRUE) # Launch the RedeR application startRedeR() resetRedeR()  # Send 'gtoy1' to the RedeR interface addGraphToRedeR(gtoy1, unit="npc") relaxRedeR()  # Fetch 'gtoy1' with a fresh layout gtoy1_2 <- getGraphFromRedeR(unit="npc")  # Check the round trip... plotGraphSpace(gtoy1_2, add.labels = TRUE)  ## Note that for the round trip, shapes and line types are ## partially compatible between ggplot2 and RedeR.  # ...alternatively, just update the graph layout gtoy1_2 <- updateLayoutFromRedeR(g=gtoy1)  # ...check the updated layout plotGraphSpace(gtoy1_2, add.labels = TRUE)

title: Interactive visualization
text: Large networks often present layout challenges. scenarios, automated static layouts may resolve visual clutter, making interactive adjustments essential graph clarity spatial organization. following example demonstrates layout large modular graph using combination automated interactive refinement.

title: Interactive visualization
text: first generate sample network predefined modules simulate cluttered system. provides baseline evaluate combination global layout algorithms local manual adjustments.
code: # Make a large modular graph nmod = 20 size = 50 gtoy2 <- sample_islands(   islands.n = nmod,     # number of modules   islands.size = size,  # nodes per module   islands.pin = 0.25,   # edges within modules (prob)   n.inter = 5)          # edges between modules  # Assign module membership V(gtoy2)$module <- rep(seq_len(nmod), each = size)  # Assign colors and node size V(gtoy2)$nodeColor <- rainbow(nmod)[V(gtoy2)$module] V(gtoy2)$nodeSize <- 2    # Initialize a 'GraphSpace', using 'layout_with_kk' to  # generate initial global coordinates gs_gtoy2 <- GraphSpace(gtoy2, layout = layout_with_kk(gtoy2))  plotGraphSpace(gs_gtoy2)

title: Interoperability with 'ggraph' and 'sf'
code: # Check required packages for this vignette if(!require("sf", quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages("sf") } if(!require("maps", quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages("maps") } if(!require("geometry", quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages("geometry") } if(!require("rnaturalearth", quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages("rnaturalearth") } if(!require("tidygraph", quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages("tidygraph") } if(!require("ggraph", quietly = TRUE)){   install.packages("ggraph") } # Load packages library("RGraphSpace") library("igraph") library("sf") library("maps") library("geometry") library("rnaturalearth") library("tidygraph") library("ggraph")

title: Interoperability with 'ggraph' and 'sf'
text: following example demonstrates interoperability RGraphSpace ggraph using igraph tidygraph objects, managing spatial data sf, standard infrastructure spatial data analysis R (Pebesma Bivand 2023). Integrating network structures spatial data often creates headache mismatched coordinate systems scales, makes example particularly interesting showcase packages handle complexity. Next, build spatial network cities; RGraphSpace geoms plugged ggraph sf workflows.
code: # Load a map and transform projection map_sf <- ne_countries(country = "Brazil", returnclass = "sf") map_proj <- st_transform(map_sf)  # Filter major cities by regional capitals data(world.cities, package = "maps") r_capitals <- c(   "Aracaju", "Belem", "Belo Horizonte", "Boa Vista", "Brasilia",    "Campo Grande", "Cuiaba", "Curitiba", "Florianopolis", "Fortaleza",    "Goiania", "Joao Pessoa", "Macapa", "Maceio", "Manaus", "Natal",    "Palmas", "Porto Alegre", "Porto Velho", "Recife", "Rio Branco",    "Rio de Janeiro", "Salvador", "Sao Luis", "Sao Paulo", "Teresina",    "Vitoria" ) cities <- subset(world.cities, country.etc == "Brazil" &      name %in% r_capitals & pop > 1200000)  # Create Delaunay triangulation edges # Note: the edges hold no particular meaning beyond # demonstrating integration between coordinate systems tri <- delaunayn(cities[,c("lat","long")]) edges <- unique(rbind(tri[,c(1,2)], tri[,c(2,3)], tri[,c(1,3)] ))  # Build an 'igraph' using city coordinates igraph_cities <- igraph::graph_from_edgelist(edges, directed = FALSE) igraph::V(igraph_cities)$x <- cities$long igraph::V(igraph_cities)$y <- cities$lat igraph::V(igraph_cities)$Cities <- cities$name igraph::V(igraph_cities)$`Population (M)` <- cities$pop/1000000 igraph::E(igraph_cities)$arrowType <- 3

title: Interoperability with 'ggraph' and 'sf'
text: following options produce visual output, demonstrating packages integrate different types input data.
code: # Option 1: Passing an 'igraph' object directly to the geoms ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = map_proj, fill = "grey95", color = "grey60") +   geom_edgespace(color = "grey40", arrow_size = 0.5, data = igraph_cities) +   geom_nodespace(aes(fill = Cities, size = `Population (M)`), data = igraph_cities) +   scale_size(range = c(3, 9)) +   inject_nodespace() +    theme_gray() +   theme_gspace_legend(key_fill = TRUE)  # Option 2: Passing a 'tbl_graph' object gr <- as_tbl_graph(igraph_cities) ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = map_proj, fill = "grey95", color = "grey60") +   geom_edgespace(color = "grey40", arrow_size = 0.5, data = gr) +   geom_nodespace(aes(fill = Cities, size = `Population (M)`), data = gr) +   scale_size(range = c(3, 9)) +   inject_nodespace() +    theme_gray() +   theme_gspace_legend(key_fill = TRUE)  # Option 3: Integration within a 'ggraph' workflow gr <- as_tbl_graph(igraph_cities) ggraph(graph = gr, x= gr$x, y = gr$y) +   geom_sf(data = map_proj, fill = "grey95", color = "grey60") +   geom_edgespace(color = "grey40", arrow_size = 0.5) +   geom_nodespace(aes(fill = Cities, size = `Population (M)`)) +   scale_size(range = c(3, 9)) +   inject_nodespace() +    theme_gray() +   theme_gspace_legend(key_fill = TRUE)  # Option 4: Passing a native 'GraphSpace' object gs <- GraphSpace(igraph_cities) ggplot(gs) +   geom_sf(data = map_proj, fill = "grey95", color = "grey60") +   geom_edgespace(color = "grey40", arrow_size = 0.5) +   geom_nodespace(aes(fill = Cities, size = `Population (M)`)) +   scale_size(range = c(3, 9)) +   inject_nodespace() +    theme_gray() +   theme_gspace_legend(key_fill = TRUE)

title: Mapping graphs to images
text: example, next extract features volcano matrix map graph nodes. graph visualized image matrix.
code: library("igraph") library("ggplot2") library("RGraphSpace")  # Extract pixel coordinates for a specific intensity quantile. coords <- which(volcano == quantile(volcano, 0.85), arr.ind = TRUE)  # Mark target pixels with '0'; it will appear as black in the background.  # This creates a visual anchor to verify the alignment precision. volcano2 <- volcano volcano2[coords] <- 0  # Create an igraph object from the pixel coordinates;  # note that at this stage, 'y' represents matrix row indices. gtoy3 <- igraph::make_empty_graph(n = nrow(coords)) igraph::V(gtoy3)$y <- coords[,1] igraph::V(gtoy3)$x <- coords[,2]  # Highlight the bottom-row vertex (max 'y' index) to demonstrate alignment;  # since matrix indexing is top-down, this accounts for the default flip  # between matrix and plot coordinate systems. igraph::V(gtoy3)$nodeColor <- NA bottom_row <- which.max(igraph::V(gtoy3)$y) igraph::V(gtoy3)$nodeColor[bottom_row] <- adjustcolor("red", 0.4)

title: Mapping graphs to images
text: Observe nodes (open circles) precisely aligned pixels (dark squares) mapped. plot, nodes remain proportional viewport regardless final output resolution. behavior especially critical network elements represent image features specific positions must stay anchored features, avoiding overlap adjacent, unrelated areas.  Note image alignment: Proper spatial alignment nodes background image requires consistent coordinate conventions. Spatial misalignment may occur input image node coordinates differ axis orientation (e.g., top-left versus bottom-left origins). accommodate differences, normalizeGraphSpace() provides orientation controls rotate.xy, flip.x, flip.y arguments. nodes appear misaligned input image, try combinations parameters correct alignment. Alternatively, try flip.v flip.h arguments apply flipping directly background image.
code: # Initialize a GraphSpace object gs <- GraphSpace(gtoy3)  # Map graph coordinates to the image space; by default, # 'y' row indices will be flipped (see comments below). gs <- normalizeGraphSpace(gs, image = as_colorraster(volcano2) ) # Render the graph with the raster as background plotGraphSpace(gs, add.image = TRUE)

title: Fine-tuning scales and offsets
text: , construct star-like network varying node sizes show geometries stay synchronized across wide range values.
code: library("igraph") library("ggplot2") library("RGraphSpace")  # Make a toy graph gtoy_star <- make_star(20, mode="out")  # Add a numeric variable V(gtoy_star)$num_var <- seq_len(vcount(gtoy_star)) / 2  # Set the 'nodeSize' attribute V(gtoy_star)$nodeSize <- seq_len(vcount(gtoy_star)) * 2  # Set node and edge colors V(gtoy_star)$nodeColor <- adjustcolor("blue", 0.1) E(gtoy_star)$edgeLineColor <- "darkred"  # Assign random arrow types, either '-->' or '--|' E(gtoy_star)$arrowType <- sample(c(1, -1), ecount(gtoy_star), replace = T)  # Make a 'GraphSpace' gs_star <- GraphSpace(gtoy_star, layout = layout_as_star(gtoy_star))

title: Fine-tuning scales and offsets
text: first example, GraphSpace object provides graph attributes. Using predefined node sizes allows consistent arrow offsets, network elements scaled npc (Normalized Parent Coordinates) units. matter plotting area resized, nodes, edges, arrows remain proportional viewport. behavior especially useful overlaying networks top reference images (photomicrography medical scans), nodes must stay locked specific pixel positions regardless output resolution.  map node size variable (like num_var), ggplot2 rescales values target range (e.g., c(2, 40)). provides advantages ggplot2 ecosystem, flexible graphical scaling coordinated legends. , however, subtle trade-keep mind. node size mapped via aes(), ggplot2 treats fixed physical dimension (usually mm) maintain consistency legends. means node size stay locked legends longer scale proportionally plotting area resized. example , geom_graphspace() handles bulk edge adjustment, arrow_offset parameter providing additional manual fine-tuning.
code: ggplot() +    geom_graphspace(data = gs_star) +    theme_gspace_coords() ggplot() +    geom_graphspace(mapping = aes(size = num_var),     data = gs_star,  arrow_offset = 0.03) +    scale_size(range = c(2, 40)) +    theme_gspace_coords() +    theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Fine-tuning scales and offsets
text: geom_graphspace() convenient quick, coordinated plots, limitation: mapping argument reserved node aesthetics. overcome limitation, can call geom_edgespace() geom_nodespace() independent layers. allows total flexibility mapping different variables edges nodes. However, layers now independent, longer “talk” default. change node size scale, edge layer won’t know needs adjust offsets accommodate new node boundaries. address , inject_nodespace() acts post-processing synchronizer: intercepts calculated sizes node layer, derive scaling rules, “injects” edge layer.  provide final note customization, scaling trade-offs apply size passed node aesthetic mapping. Otherwise, RGraphSpace defaults using npc units network elements.
code: # We shuffle 'num_var' to demonstrate that edges  # still find their specific boundaries set.seed(234) gs_star$num_var2 <- sample(gs_star$num_var)  # Execute the plot, calling 'inject_nodespace' last  # to capture the final layer states ggplot(data = gs_star) +    geom_edgespace(arrow_offset = 0.03) +    geom_nodespace(mapping = aes(size = num_var2 )) +    scale_size(range = c(2, 40)) +   inject_nodespace() +   theme_gspace_coords() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: A Lightweight Interface Between igraph and ggplot2 Graphics
text: RGraphSpace R package generates ggplot2 graphics igraph objects (Csardi Nepusz 2006), scaling nodes edges unit space. package implements new ggplot2 prototypes (Wickham 2016), optimized representing large networks. enables extensive customization aesthetics visual style, including interoperability ggraph tidygraph workflows (Pedersen 2024). Three specialized geoms translate graph data geometric layers. geom_graphspace(): high-level convenience layer processes nodes edges single call. geom_nodespace(): Dedicated rendering nodes. Inherits GeomPoint aesthetic mappings, modified inform edge layer node states. geom_edgespace(): Handles relational data nodes. Inherits GeomSegment aesthetic mappings; unlike standard segments, “node-aware” dynamically calibrates start end points connected nodes.

title: Create a GraphSpace object — GraphSpace,ANY-method
text: GraphSpace objects designed bridge gap network analysis (via igraph) high-quality visualization (via ggplot2). constructor ensures necessary aesthetics geom_graphspace pre-processed validated. Coordinate System Normalization: default, constructor expects coordinates x y vertex attributes, along unique IDs name vertex attribute. provided, constructor generate sequential IDs assign layout using layout_nicely function. coordinates define relative positioning nodes. optimal rendering, recommended pass object normalizeGraphSpace construction. converts vertex positions Normalized Parent Coordinates (NPC), ensuring graph remains centered scaled relative plotting area. Data Structure: resulting object stores nodes edges separate internal slots, preserving metadata nodeSize edgeLineColor. igraph object provided without specific styling attributes, GraphSpace assign default values defined geom_graphspace aesthetics. Users can also specify custom variables input graph used aesthetics within ggplot2 grammar. Arrowhead Mapping: arrowType attribute (see Arrowhead types section) allows mapping symbolic aliases ("-->") internal integer codes. useful assigning interaction types directed undirected graphs (e.g., activation vs. inhibition).

text: interface integrate 'igraph' 'ggplot2' graphics within normalized coordinate system. 'RGraphSpace' implements geometric objects based 'ggplot2' prototypes, optimized representation large networks. package provides three specialized 'geoms' translate graph data geometric layers, supporting customization aesthetics visual styles. 'geoms' use dual-anchor normalization approach align layers, required analyses network elements must referenced spatial map. 'RGraphSpace' aims facilitate side--side visualization multiple graphs spatially aligned reference maps images.

code: library(RGraphSpace) library(igraph) library(ggplot2)  # Make a demo igraph gtoy1 <- make_star(15, mode="out")  # Set some node attributes V(gtoy1)$nodeSize <- runif(vcount(gtoy1), 1, 20) V(gtoy1)$nodeColor <- rainbow(vcount(gtoy1))  # Set some variables V(gtoy1)$user_var1 <- runif(vcount(gtoy1), 1, 3)^3 V(gtoy1)$user_var2 <-  rep(c(1, 2, 3), each = 5)  # Create a GraphSpace object gs <- GraphSpace(gtoy1, layout = layout_in_circle(gtoy1)) #> Validating the 'igraph' object... #> Vertex attribute 'name' missing; assigning names...  #> Creating a 'GraphSpace' object...  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Example 1: Nodes scaling with the legend # When 'size' is mapped inside aes(), it follows # ggplot2 default behavior: size is translated  # to absolute units (mm) via 'scale_size()'.  ggplot() +    geom_graphspace(   mapping = aes(size = nodeSize, fill = user_var2),    data = gs, arrow_offset = 0.01) +    scale_size(range = c(1, 12)) +    theme(aspect.ratio = 1)    # Example 2: Nodes scaling with the viewport # When 'size' is passed as a node attribute,  # inherited from the igraph object, it is # interpreted as a percentage of the plotting  # area and translated to NPC units.  ggplot() +   geom_graphspace(mapping = aes(fill = user_var2),    data = gs, arrow_offset = 0.01) +    theme(aspect.ratio = 1)    } # }

code: library(RGraphSpace) library(igraph) library(ggplot2)  # Make a demo igraph gtoy1 <- make_star(15, mode="out")  # Set some node attributes V(gtoy1)$nodeSize <- runif(vcount(gtoy1), 1, 20) V(gtoy1)$nodeColor <- rainbow(vcount(gtoy1))  # Set some variables V(gtoy1)$user_var1 <- runif(vcount(gtoy1), 1, 3)^3 V(gtoy1)$user_var2 <-  rep(c(1, 2, 3), each = 5)  # Create a GraphSpace object gs <- GraphSpace(gtoy1, layout = layout_in_circle(gtoy1)) #> Validating the 'igraph' object... #> Vertex attribute 'name' missing; assigning names...  #> Creating a 'GraphSpace' object...  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Example 1: Nodes scaling with the legend # When 'size' is mapped inside aes(), it follows # ggplot2 default behavior: size is translated  # to absolute units (mm) via 'scale_size()'.  ggplot() +  geom_edgespace(data = gs, arrow_offset = 0.01) +   geom_nodespace(mapping = aes(size = nodeSize, fill = user_var2),    data = gs) +    scale_size(range = c(1, 12)) +    theme(aspect.ratio = 1)    # Example 2: Nodes scaling with the viewport # When 'size' is passed as a node attribute,  # inherited from the igraph object, it is  # interpreted as a percentage of the plotting  # area and translated to NPC units.  ggplot() +  geom_edgespace(data = gs, arrow_offset = 0.01) + geom_nodespace(mapping = aes(fill = user_var2), data = gs) + theme(aspect.ratio = 1)    } # }

title: Dynamic Scale Injection for Edge Clipping — inject_nodespace
text: function operates two stages within ggplot2 workflow: Capture: scans plot layers GeomNodeSpace extract mapping variables (aes()) static parameters (specifically size stroke). Injection: locates GeomEdgeSpace layers injects scale rules, captured mappings, fixed parameters geometry parameters. "lazy injection" calculates edge clipping based actual scales used nodes, even scales defined layers. Note: inject_nodespace() must called last ggplot chain allow function correctly scan previously added layers scales.

title: Dynamic Scale Injection for Edge Clipping — inject_nodespace
code: library(RGraphSpace) library(igraph) library(ggplot2)  # Generate a toy star graph gtoy1 <- make_star(15, mode="out")  # Set node and edge attributes V(gtoy1)$my_node_var <- runif(vcount(gtoy1), 1, 20) E(gtoy1)$my_edge_var <-  runif(ecount(gtoy1), 1, 20)  # Create a GraphSpace object with a circular layout gs <- GraphSpace(gtoy1, layout = layout_in_circle(gtoy1)) #> Validating the 'igraph' object... #> Vertex attribute 'name' missing; assigning names...  #> Creating a 'GraphSpace' object...  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Build the plot # Note that inject_nodespace() is called at the end to # synchronize node sizes with edge clipping. ggplot() +   geom_edgespace(aes(colour = my_edge_var), data = gs) +   geom_nodespace(aes(size = my_node_var), data = gs) +   scale_size(range = c(2, 15)) +   inject_nodespace() } # }
